patient.chief_complaint: marked leukocytosis: WBC commonly 50,000-300,000/uL with left-shifted differential (myelocytes, metamyelocytes, bands)
symptoms: marked leukocytosis: WBC commonly 50,000-300,000/uL with left-shifted differential (myelocytes, metamyelocytes, bands)
absolute basophilia (present in virtually 100% of CML cases; absent in leukemoid reactions)
fatigue, night sweats, and weight loss from hypermetabolic state driven by massive cell turnover
thrombocytosis (platelet count 400,000-600,000/uL in ~30-50% of patients at presentation)
incidental discovery on routine CBC in an asymptomatic patient (~30-40% of cases in the TKI era)
signs: splenomegaly causing early satiety, left upper quadrant fullness, or dragging discomfort (present in ~50-70% at diagnosis)
